introSection: Superman: Ride of Steel was a steel hyper roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Built by Intamin, it was a mirror-image of Ride of Steel at Six Flags Darien Lake, which opened in 1999.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was announced in March 2000. Superman: Ride of Steel opened on May 13, 2000.

In 2004, Superman: Ride of Steel was closed indefinitely when a 55-year-old Connecticut man fell to his death from Superman The Ride at Six Flags New England.

In 2016, the ride received virtual reality headsets which played a Superman-themed film, similarly to other rides in the Six Flags chain.

Superman: Ride of Steel was given a single rider line for the 2022 season.

For the 2025 season, the ride received a shipment of new trains from Intamin. However, Superman: Ride of Steel later closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

In late December 2025, the trains from the ride were spotted on site at Six Flags Darien Lake in Buffalo, New York, USA.

sections.title: Theming
sections.content: The font used on the ride implies a vague tie-in to Superman: The Animated Series, which was popular around the time of the ride's opening.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Once the train has been dispatched from the station, it makes a winding, 180-degree turn. The train ascends up the chain lift hill and then drops  at an angle of 68 degrees. The train reaches the maximum speed of before entering a sharp turn and the first hill. A 540-degree helix follows, in addition to another smaller hill. The finale involves a 500-degree helix and several more airtime hills before reaching the brake run and returning to the station.

fullText:
Superman: Ride of Steel was a steel hyper roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Built by Intamin, it was a mirror-image of Ride of Steel at Six Flags Darien Lake, which opened in 1999.

The roller coaster was announced in March 2000. Superman: Ride of Steel opened on May 13, 2000.

In 2004, Superman: Ride of Steel was closed indefinitely when a 55-year-old Connecticut man fell to his death from Superman The Ride at Six Flags New England.

In 2016, the ride received virtual reality headsets which played a Superman-themed film, similarly to other rides in the Six Flags chain.

Superman: Ride of Steel was given a single rider line for the 2022 season.

For the 2025 season, the ride received a shipment of new trains from Intamin. However, Superman: Ride of Steel later closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

In late December 2025, the trains from the ride were spotted on site at Six Flags Darien Lake in Buffalo, New York, USA.



The font used on the ride implies a vague tie-in to Superman: The Animated Series, which was popular around the time of the ride's opening.

Red track and blue supports.

Once the train has been dispatched from the station, it makes a winding, 180-degree turn. The train ascends up the chain lift hill and then drops  at an angle of 68 degrees. The train reaches the maximum speed of before entering a sharp turn and the first hill. A 540-degree helix follows, in addition to another smaller hill. The finale involves a 500-degree helix and several more airtime hills before reaching the brake run and returning to the station.
